Fixture chrome

Comment Heavy Fixture

This fixture keeps the article body clearly readable while the surrounding main container also contains a large discussion block.

The root selector should still resolve to the article because comment chrome should not win the score just by being verbose.

Why It Matters

Real pages rarely fail because the article disappears completely; they fail because noisy chrome makes the whole app shell look like the safer root.

This regression keeps the browser harness honest by checking the same heuristic under a real page and a real CDP session.

Discussion

Comment 1: this discussion block is intentionally long and repetitive so the enclosing main container would otherwise outscore the actual article root.

Comment 2: the text volume here exists to stress the selector heuristics rather than to simulate meaningful conversation.

Comment 3: the root scorer should not confuse a noisy supplement block with the reading surface just because the supplement is longer.

Comment 4: if this fixture regresses, the browser boundary test should catch it before users notice that exports include the wrong container.

Comment 5: repeated content keeps the discussion section heavy enough to matter in the scoring path.

Comment 6: production fixtures should be explicit and reviewable instead of being generated inside test helpers.

Comment 7: that is the whole point of moving this surface to file-backed HTML.

Comment 8: the browser harness now exercises a case that previously only existed in a unit test string literal.
